metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_70
metadata.comment:
Rationale: It is demonstrated that a PKC alpha knockout has a reduction in post-tetanic potentiation (PTP), in the Calyx of Held. It is also shown that PTP operates at least partly due to a potentiation in readily releasable pool size, and that this potentiation is diminished in the PKC alpha/beta knockout.

It should be noted that in some synapses, PKC alpha is not required for PTP (Wang et.al. 2016).. Experimental description: Robust PTP was observed in wild-type mice. (...) Representative experiments show that somewhat smaller PTP was observed in PKCα-/- animals (Figure 2B), and PTP was greatly reduced in PKCβ-/- (Figure 2C) and PKCα-/-β-/- mice (Figure 2D).
